items.title: Trump says he has "no problem" with Russian tanker bringing oil to Cuba
items.standfirst: When asked if a New York Times report that the tanker would be allowed to reach Cuba was true, Mr. Trump said: "If a country wants to send some oil into Cuba right now, I have no problem whether it's Russia or not."
items.content: When asked if a New York Times report that the tanker would be allowed to reach Cuba was true, Mr. Trump said: "If a country wants to send some oil into Cuba right now, I have no problem whether it's Russia or not."

items.title: Russian tanker nears blockaded Cuba, Trump shrugs off ‚Äòa boat of oil‚Äô
items.standfirst: A ‚ÄãRussian oil tanker entered Cuban waters on Monday despite a de facto US fuel blockade as President Donald Trump has turned up the political pressure on ‚Äåthe Caribbean island in recent days. But on Sunday, Trump appeared to relax his tone, claiming the Russian tanker was just "a boat of oil" and its cargo of 730,000 barrels of crude was "not going to matter".
items.content: A ‚ÄãRussian oil tanker entered Cuban waters on Monday despite a de facto US fuel blockade as President Donald Trump has turned up the political pressure on ‚Äåthe Caribbean island in recent days. items.standfirst: US president tells Financial Times his ‚Äòpreference would be to take the oil‚Äô but that ‚Äòsome stupid people back in the US say: ‚Äúwhy are you doing that?‚Äù‚ÄôFull report: Iran accuses US of plotting ground assault while publicly seeking talksAnalysis: what the Houthis‚Äô entry into the Iran war means for the conflict and the wider regionDonald Trump is weighing a military operation to extract nearly 1,000 pounds (454kg) of uranium from Iran, the Wall Street Journal is reporting, citing unnamed US officials.The mission would likely put American forces inside the country for days or longer, the report says.But the president remains generally open to the idea, according to the officials, because it could help accomplish his central goal of preventing Iran from ever making a nuclear weapon.The combined effect of both waterways being shut to commercial traffic from countries that neither the Iranians nor Houthis favour would be devastating.Napoleon Bonaparte‚Äôs remark that ‚Äúthe policy of a state lies in its geography‚Äù has never seemed more apt. items.title: Iran accuses US of plotting ground assault while publicly seeking talks
items.standfirst: Tehran says it will confront any land attack, as Trump says regime‚Äôs export hub on Kharg island could be taken ‚Äòvery easily‚ÄôMiddle East crisis ‚Äì live updatesIran has warned the US that it is prepared to confront any ground assault, accusing Washington of secretly planning a land attack while publicly seeking talks, as the war that has killed thousands of people and caused the biggest ever disruption to global energy supplies entered its second month.In a message published to mark 30 days since the start of the war, the Iranian parliament speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, said: ‚ÄúThe enemy signals negotiation in public, while in secret it plots a ground attack.‚Äù Continue reading...

items.title: What the Houthis‚Äô entry into the Iran war means for the conflict and the wider region
items.standfirst: Fresh attacks on Red Sea shipping would be devastating ‚Äì but the Iranian proxy has reasons to be cautiousMiddle East crisis ‚Äì live updatesThe true significance of the long-awaited entry of Yemen‚Äôs Houthis into the Iran war depends on whether the Tehran-backed proxy group is intending to send a few missiles and drones from a distance towards Israel or will instead capitalise on its proximity to the narrow Bab al-Mandab strait to effectively close off the Red Sea to shipping, just as Iran has in effect shut the strait of Hormuz.The combined effect of both waterways being shut to commercial traffic from countries that neither the Iranians nor Houthis favour would be devastating. Napoleon Bonaparte‚Äôs remark that ‚Äúthe policy of a state lies in its geography‚Äù has never seemed more apt. Continue reading...

items.title: Iran: 3.500 US troops arrive in the region
items.standfirst: Iran will target the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier "as soon as" it comes within range, the country's navy chief said Sunday. Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf earlier accused Washington of plotting a ground attack on Iran even as it claims to be engaging in diplomatic efforts. Senior fellow in Geopolitics, Royal United Services Institute, Dr. H.A. Hellyer shares further insights.
items.content: Iran will target the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier "as soon as" it comes within range, the country's navy chief said Sunday. Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf earlier accused Washington of plotting a ground attack on Iran even as it claims to be engaging in diplomatic efforts. Senior fellow in Geopolitics, Royal United Services Institute, Dr. H.A. Hellyer shares further insights.

items.title: Iran says it's 'waiting' for possible U.S. ground assault as 3,500 troops arrive in Gulf
items.standfirst: Iran said Sunday it is ready to face U.S. troops on the ground, accusing Washington of secretly planning an assault while seeking negotiations to end the war.‚ÄúThe enemy, openly, sends messages of negotiation and dialogue, but secretly is planning a ground attack,‚Äù said Iran‚Äôs parliament speaker, Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, according to state media outlet IRNA and state-affiliated Tasnim
items.content: Iran said Sunday it is ready to face U.S. troops on the ground, accusing Washington of secretly planning an assault while seeking negotiations to end the war.‚ÄúThe enemy, openly, sends messages of negotiation and dialogue, but secretly is planning a ground attack,‚Äù said Iran‚Äôs parliament speaker, Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf, according to state media outlet IRNA and state-affiliated Tasnim

Reported by France24, BBC World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Israel restores Latin Patriarch‚Äôs access to Holy Sepulchre church after international outcry
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem now has "full and immediate access" to the Church of the Holy Sepulchre after Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa was blocked from holding Palm Sunday Mass at Christianity's holiest site, triggering an international outcry.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem now has "full and immediate access" to the Church of the Holy Sepulchre after Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa was blocked from holding Palm Sunday Mass at Christianity's holiest site, triggering an international outcry.

items.content: Christians in Jerusalem marked Holy Week under wartime restrictions as Israeli police blocked the Latin Patriarch.

items.title: Israeli police bar top Catholic figure in Jerusalem from holding Palm Sunday mass at Holy Sepulchre
items.standfirst: Israeli police prevented the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre to celebrate Palm Sunday mass, according to the Latin Patriarchate of Jerusalem. It is not yet clear whether this blockade was intentional or a mistake from the Israeli police; in the meantime, some world leaders, including Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and French President Emmanuel Macron reacted condemning the event as an "offense" to faithful. France 24 correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky reports the latest from Jerusalem.
items.content: Israeli police prevented the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem from entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre to celebrate Palm Sunday mass, according to the Latin Patriarchate of Jerusalem. It is not yet clear whether this blockade was intentional or a mistake from the Israeli police; in the meantime, some world leaders, including Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and French President Emmanuel Macron reacted condemning the event as an "offense" to faithful. France 24 correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky reports the latest from Jerusalem.

items.title: DHS funding freeze now longest partial government shutdown in US history
items.standfirst: If the now six-week partial shutdown continues after the weekend, it will become the longest of any shutdownThe shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), the fourth-largest agency in the US government, became the longest partial shutdown in US history on Sunday.If the now six-week partial shutdown continues after the weekend, it will also become the longest of any shutdown, surpassing the impasse late last year that dragged on for 43 days. Continue reading...

items.title: Massive protests against Trump across US
items.standfirst: Huge crowds protested Saturday against US President Donald Trump's actions and the war in Iran in "No Kings" rallies across the US and in Europe. Minnesota took centre stage, with thousands of people standing shoulder-to-shoulder to celebrate resistance to Trump's aggressive immigration enforcement. France 24 International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
items.content: Huge crowds protested Saturday against US President Donald Trump's actions and the war in Iran in "No Kings" rallies across the US and in Europe. France 24 International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

clusters.title: Lebanon mourns three journalists killed in Israeli strikes
clusters.neutral_headline: Hundreds in Beirut mourn journalists killed in Israeli strike
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Lebanon mourns three journalists killed in Israeli strikes
items.standfirst: Hundreds of mourners gathered at a cemetery south of Beirut on Sunday to attend the funeral of journalists Ali Shoeib, Fatima Ftouni, and Mohammed Ftouni, who were killed in an Israeli strike a day earlier. Israel has acknowledged carrying out the attack, claiming that Shoeib was affiliated with a militant group, but the incident has sparked widespread outrage within Lebanon, where officials have condemned it as a clear breach of international law. According to the Committee to Protect Journalists, the deaths bring the number of media workers killed in Lebanon since the start of the Israel‚ÄìGaza war to eleven, as Eliza Herbert reports.
items.content: Hundreds of mourners gathered at a cemetery south of Beirut on Sunday to attend the funeral of journalists Ali Shoeib, Fatima Ftouni, and Mohammed Ftouni, who were killed in an Israeli strike a day earlier. Israel has acknowledged carrying out the attack, claiming that Shoeib was affiliated with a militant group, but the incident has sparked widespread outrage within Lebanon, where officials have condemned it as a clear breach of international law. According to the Committee to Protect Journalists, the deaths bring the number of media workers killed in Lebanon since the start of the Israel‚ÄìGaza war to eleven, as Eliza Herbert reports.
